title: The last hurrah: Where American travellers are spending Labor Day weekend
content:
Seeing Shakespeare in New York, Bright Eyes in Seattle and Roald Dahl's Enormous Crocodile in London – these five destinations are topping lists for the last holiday of summer.

For many Americans, Labor Day marks the unofficial end of summer; a long weekend for parades, barbecues and one last getaway before the school year begins. According to the American Automobile Association (AAA), the Transportation Security Administration expects17.4 million peopleto travel between 28 August and 3 September, up from 17.2 million last year. Data from travel sites includingAAA,Dollar Flight ClubandKayakshow that domestic travel is up significantly, likely due to the fact that domestic flights are the cheapest they've been in five years.

"This Labor Day we're seeing one of the biggest splits in pricing we've ever tracked," says Jesse Neugarten, founder and CEO of Dollar Flight Club. "Domestic flights are about 5% lower than last year and nearly 20% lower than 2019. On the flip side, international fares are still sky-high."

According to Neugarten, international fares are two to three times higher on average, while domestic Labor Day fares are currently at a five-year low, with New York, Seattle and Boston being some of the most affordable US destinations overall.

Even so, Americans aren't just staying close to home. There are several international destinations showing up on Labor Day travel hot lists, with Rome and London ranking among the most affordable this year, according to Dollar Flight Club.

"Labor Day caps off summer travel and provides one final holiday weekend before the fall routine begins," Stacey Barber, Vice President of AAA Travel, said in a statement.

Here are the five top destinations Americans are choosing for their last hurrah of summer 2025.

The Big Apple remains one of the busiest Labor Day hubs, with Kayak noting it as one of the most popular destinations this time of year. That's no surprise as theUS Opencontinues this weekend, and theWest Indian Paradethunders through Brooklyn on 1 September. At an average $263 flight cost – down 2% from last year – it's also a relative bargain. Beyond the parade, the freeShakespeare in the Parkfestival is back this summer after a renovation hiatus, with a starry lineup including Sandra Oh, Peter Dinklage and Lupita Nyong'o.

Seattle is a top choice for music lovers this Labor Day weekend, and the top domestic choice overall according to AAA. This year's edition of theKitsap Blues Music Festivalbrings artists including Christone "Kingfish" Ingram and Eric Gales; whileBumbershoot, Seattle's largest music and arts festival, features Bright Eyes, Janelle Monáe and Tank and the Bangas. Expect a citywide celebration of art, music, food and culture.

Boston shows up on all three lists as one of the most popular and affordable destinations this Labor Day, and as of 28 August it's reachable by what Amtrak is calling its "fastest passenger train in the US",the NextGen Acela, which reaches 160mph and connects Boston to New York, Washington DC and various points between. On 1 September, the city is also hosting its first Labor Day parade which will see marchers organised by the Greater Boston Labor Council stroll from the State House to City Hall Plaza. TheLawn on D, an open-air community space, will have a family-friendly celebration with lawn games and live music all weekend. Baseball lovers should catch a game at the legendaryFenway Park, where the Chicago Cubs will face the Pittsburgh Pirates this weekend.

For those still dreaming of heading overseas, London is among the most affordable international destinations for Americans, with flights averaging $687, down 5% from last year.

Once there, visitors can take in Brigadoon and Roald Dahl's The Enormous Crocodile atRegent's Park Open Air Theatre, or tourBuckingham Palacebefore its magnificent State Rooms close to the public on 28 September. This year is also the200th anniversary of the modern railway, and the London Transport Museum is offering a series of family-friendlyactivities and workshopsto highlight the milestone.

Despitesummer heat– and recentovertourismprotests – Rome remains high on Americans' bucket lists. Bookings to the Italian capital are up this year, with more than30 million visitors, including many Americans, flocking to the city for theCatholic Jubilee. Labor Day travellers can enjoy music, theatre and cultural exhibitions at theEstate Romana festival, which takes place on the weekends from 15 June until 15 October; or catch a concert at theRoma Summer Fest, a citywide music festival that runs into September.
